text:
There once was a man named Alfred. Alfred was a painter. He lived in a small village on a rugged coast. Every morning he would paint. He painted landscapes so vivid you could almost smell the sea.

One day Alfred decided to paint something different. He wanted to paint sea creatures. He had never done that before. He put his paintbrush to the water glass.

His first idea was a mermaid. She was beautiful. It was a silly thing to paint, maybe even silly to want to do, but somewhere deep down Alfred felt it was right.

After a few strokes, Alfred painted a dragon. A huge, fire-breathing creature that looked incredibly real.

Later, he did a painting of a sea lion walking on land. That one seemed to pop right out of the canvas.

Then he painted a sea turtle. It was small and sweet, like the oldest friend you've ever had.

Finally, he painted a grumpy old lobster diving deep into the ocean. That image he
